raw.objective: “to assess whether the MM+FO model provides a better fit to the phenytoin data than the Michaelis-Menten (MM) model (i.e. whether a linear pathway is present in parallel to a nonlinear pathway in the elimination of phenytoin). The second objective was to ascertain whether including a linear pathway results in a meaningful improvement in the prediction of the dose-concentration relationship”
raw.covariates: Tested: - Included: race smoking status, age on VM
raw.observations: Same data was used as in (Valodia 1999 10051055). The linear portion of elimination improved the description of the data compared only including non-linear kinetics. It is unclear why the parameters are different from those reported earlier, given that the model is the same.
raw.structure: 1 compartment model with parallel michaelis menten and linear elimination
raw.equations: Vm = (theta1*WT+theta3)*(theta4**RACE)*(theta5**SMK)*(theta7**ELD)*exp(eta1) Km = theta2*exp(eta2) CL = theta6*exp(eta3) Css = -(1/2)*(((Vm/CL)+Km-(DD/CL))-sqrt((((Vm/CL)+Km-(DD/CL))**2)+((4*DD*Km)/CL))) RACE is race (0 if black; 1 if colored) SMK is smoker (0 if not smoking, 1 if smoking) AGE is age (0 if younger than 65 years, 1 if older)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=2.91 theta2=7.31 theta3=228 theta4=1.07 theta5=1.09 theta6=2.14 theta7=0.96 omega1=- omega2=- omega3=-

structural_model.description: 1 compartment model with parallel michaelis menten and linear elimination

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to assess whether the MM+FO model provides a better fit to the phenytoin data than the Michaelis-Menten (MM) model (i.e. whether a linear pathway is present in parallel to a nonlinear pathway in the elimination of phenytoin). The second objective was to ascertain whether including a linear pathway results in a meaningful improvement in the prediction of the dose-concentration relationship”

covariates.raw: Tested: - Included: race smoking status, age on VM

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
